mapping.a_element: critical switching rate separating persistence from extinction
mapping.b_element: sharp decay threshold s_c separating well-posedness from ill-posedness

mapping.a_element: sub-/super-critical regime dichotomy (species survives vs dies out)
mapping.b_element: s>s_c vs s<s_c regime dichotomy (locally well-posed vs ill-posed)

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic presence of a sharp threshold separating two regimes; the machinery differs entirely. Paper A uses averaging of alternating (switched) ODE vector fields, where a sign condition on a 'switching rescue function' built from two boundary vector fields governs persistence, whereas Paper B uses multilinear (trilinear) harmonic-analysis estimates on a four-wave resonant manifold to fix a critical Sobolev-weight exponent for PDE well-posedness. There is no role-for-role correspondence: A's two alternating environments and switching rate have no analogue in B's single collision operator, and B's resonant-manifold geometry and kernel high-frequency growth have no analogue in A.
